research_workflow.mode_overview: This mode explores and analyzes the HRM codebase without making changes. It traces data flows, identifies patterns, answers architectural questions, and produces structured findings to help developers understand the system.

frontend.framework: Vue 3 Composition API with script setup
frontend.utilities: Excel workers, loading helpers, validation utilities

step.details: Parse the user's question to identify: - What feature, flow, or pattern they want to understand - Whether they need a broad overview or a specific trace - Whether the question spans frontend, backend, or both

Based on the question, determine which files or directories to examine first: - Feature name → look for matching Vue component, API module, router, query file - Data flow → start from the user-facing component and trace inward - Pattern question → identify representative examples across modules - Bug investigation → start from the symptom location and trace dependencies

steps.step: Use semantic search to find files related to the topic
Read directory listings to understand module structure
Identify the key files that participate in the flow

phase.name: deep_reading
phase.description: Read and understand the relevant code

steps.step: Read each key file, focusing on exports, function signatures, and data transformations
Trace function calls and data flow between files
Note patterns, conventions, and any deviations

phase.name: synthesis
phase.description: Organize findings into a clear answer

steps.step: Summarize the flow or pattern with file references
Highlight key decision points, branching logic, or important details
Note any inconsistencies, gaps, or areas needing further investigation
Present findings in a structured format with clickable file references

completion_criteria.criterion: The user's question has been answered with specific file and line references
Data flows are traced end-to-end where applicable
Findings are presented in a clear, structured format
Ambiguities or gaps are explicitly noted
